findings:
## analyst-quality — GPU Extension Quality Standards (March 2026)

### Technology Radar
| Technology | Tier | Signal | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| WebGPU | ADOPT | Strong | 70% browsers (Firefox 147 Jan 2026, Safari iOS 26, Chrome/Edge v113+); 65% new apps |
| Sigma.js v3 (WebGL) | ADOPT | Stable | Production graph tools (Neo4j, Linkurious) |
| D3-force + Canvas | ADOPT | Stable | Ceiling ~5k nodes; WebGL needed for 10k+ |
| Ollama | TRIAL | Dev-only | LinkedIn/RedHat: NOT for enterprise production; vLLM is standard |
| Three.js WebGPU renderer | ADOPT | Strong | r171+ auto-WebGL fallback; two-line migration |
| Playwright + Vitest | ADOPT | Dominant | Official VS Code recommendation + community consensus |
| nomic-embed-text | TRIAL | Solid | Outperforms Ada-002; 5-14k sentences/sec CPU |
| Qwen2.5-Coder-7B | ADOPT | Strong | 95.51 tok/s on RTX 4090 Q4 |

### Performance Baselines (RTX 4090, Ollama 0.5.x, Q4 quant)
- qwen2.5-coder:7b: 95.51 tok/s, 65% VRAM
- llama3.1:8b: 95.51 tok/s, 41% VRAM
- qwen2.5:14b: 63.92 tok/s, 45% VRAM
- qwen2.5:32b: 34.39 tok/s, 90% VRAM
- Minimum interactive UX: ≥30 tok/s
- WebGPU vs WebGL compute: 15-30x faster; scene render: ~10x faster (Babylon.js)
- WebGL vs GPU-off D3-SVG: SVG cap ~500 nodes; Canvas+D3 ~2-5k; Sigma.js WebGL ~50k
- nomic-embed: 5-14k sentences/sec CPU; 8192 token context

### Quality Gates
1. GPU Feature Detection + Graceful Fallback — navigator.gpu check before init; non-GPU path provides meaningful output. Integration test with GPU disabled.
2. CSP Compliance — nonce-based CSP, no inline scripts. Automated lint in CI. Marketplace blocker.
3. VRAM Budget ≤2 GB on 8 GB device without consent. CI profiling script.
4. First Meaningful Paint ≤2s on laptop GPU (RTX 3060 class). Playwright timing assertion.
5. 60fps at nominal load (1k nodes or 50k data points). Frame timing E2E assertion.
6. Ollama failure UX — user-friendly error within 3s on unreachable Ollama. Unit test with mocked fetch failure.
7. No GPU context leak — GPUDevice.destroy() on panel dispose. Unit test + manual Electron monitor.
8. Webview message protocol type-safe — Zod schemas shared between host and webview. TS compilation + runtime validation.
9. Ollama optional — ≥50% features work without Ollama. Activation test without Ollama process.
10. CI performance trace — Playwright captures DevTools trace; asserts no long tasks >50ms during render.
11. Embedding consistency — nomic-embed output deterministic across restarts. Snapshot test cosine similarity ≥0.9999.
12. Extension startup ≤1s — scoped activationEvents (not '*'). VS Code startup profiler in CI.

### Testing Strategy
Unit (Vitest, no GPU): vi.stubGlobal navigator.gpu with mock adapter/device; test all fallback branches; 80%+ branch coverage on GPU decision logic.
Integration (@vscode/test-electron, headful): Electron 34 (VS Code 1.110) ships WebGPU; use --enable-gpu flag; test panel lifecycle and Ollama mock server.
E2E (Playwright): --no-sandbox --enable-gpu; frame rate via PerformanceObserver; visual regression ≤2% pixel diff; perf trace artifact per CI run. GPU CI requires self-hosted runner (GitHub Actions free tier has no GPU).

### Community Consensus
Agreed: WebGPU production-ready March 2026; Ollama = dev tool only; CSP compliance non-negotiable; Canvas beats SVG at >500 nodes; Vitest+Playwright is 2026 standard.
Contested: WebGPU in VS Code webviews (Electron 34 available but not officially supported by MS for extensions); no native WebGPU graph library (Sigma.js v3 is WebGL); GPU in CI (no free tier GPU runners).
recommendations: Gate 1 (GPU Fallback): implement non-GPU code path FIRST; test with GPU disabled before shipping any GPU feature.
Gate 2 (CSP): automate CSP lint in CI from day one; CSP violation is a Marketplace hard blocker.
Gate 5 (60fps): define 1k-node frame-rate Playwright assertion before first webview release.
Gate 7 (Context Leak): add GPUDevice.destroy() to panel dispose and verify in E2E; prevents Electron memory leaks.
Gate 12 (Startup ≤1s): audit activationEvents for wildcard '*' before GPU work begins; scope to onView/onCommand.
